MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
G. Franchesca Callejo for executor Jonathan Campbell, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter came on for hearing on 1) Status on Close of Estate; and 2) Status on Fund Transfer.

Ms. Callejo reported that an agreement had been reached with Ms. Hinshaw regarding their creditor's claim, and that she expects to file a partial rejection and partial allowance within the week. Ms. Callejo also reported that the accounting is largely complete and requested six weeks to finalize and file a petition for final distribution.

The Court continued the matter to October 27, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-10-2025 Continued To Mar-12-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Franchesca Callejo for Jonathan Campbell, Executor (via Zoom).
Rowena Navia for and with Rachel Pozzi, Conservator (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding the dispute over whether Ms. Pozzi should transfer all funds and later request her settlement amount ($175,000) or retain it before transfer. Ms. Navia expressed her client's preference to pay herself the settlement amount and transfer the remaining balance to the estate. Ms. Callejo argued that Ms. Pozzi is holding funds to renegotiate settlement terms and requested full transfer of funds per the settlement agreement and court order.

After hearing oral arguments, the court modified the transfer arrangement as follows: Ms. Pozzi is authorized to withhold her settlement amount ($175,000) and transfer the remaining funds. All funds must be transferred and accounts closed by March 4, 2025. A status hearing was set for March 12 ,2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, re: status on fund transfer and estate closure progress. The status on close of estate hearing is also continued to be heard on the same date. The court will issue an order as to this ruling.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-13-2025 Continued To Feb-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
G. Franchesca Callejo for and with Jonathan Campbell, Executor (via Zoom).
Rowena Navia for and with Rachel Pozzi, Conservator (via Zoom).

Ms. Callejo indicated that Chase Bank has refused to release funds directly to the Executor, citing a court order requiring Ms. Pozzi to distribute the funds. Ms. Navia argued that the executor's out-of-state residence complicated the matter and noted that there are three separate financial institutions involved. Both sides emphasized the need to resolve the distribution to finalize the estate and discharge the bond.

Ms. Pozzi proposed retaining money owed to her and transferring the remaining funds to the executor to expedite resolution. Ms. Callejo objected, insisting that the full amount should be transferred according to the court order.

The court acknowledged the dispute between the parties and the challenges presented by the banks and indicated that it would review the matter further and potentially issue an updated order addressing the issues raised, particularly regarding the authority and process for the distribution of funds. The court continued the matter to February 10, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-13-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo appeared via zoom for Jonathan Campbell (petitioner).

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary. The lost will dated December 12, 2018 is admitted to probate. Jonathan Campbell is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for November 25 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status on administration is filed two weeks prior to the hearing date, the status hearing will go off calendar. Isidoor Bornstein is appointed Probate Referee.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Barrett: Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-23-2023 Off Calendar

Verified Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Mutual General Release
Off Calendar Oct-27-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan (litigation counsel) appeared by zoom for Jonathan Campbell.
Franchesca Callejo appeared by zoom for Jonathan Campbell.
Edward Broitman appeared by zoom for and with Rachel Pozzi (in person).
Rafael Ruano appeared by zoom for Cheryl Mosbacher.

The court noted that Mr. Campbell's verification has been filed, and stated that it waives the incomplete notice on how to appear remotely and that it was hoping that Mrs. Pozzi would sign a joinder. Mr. Broitman stated that he has obtained a joinder, that there was some back and forth regarding language in the petition, that there was other hold up regarding Mrs. Mosbacher's fees, and that he can either file the joinder or he can represent to the court that is has been signed. Mr. Ruano stated that he has no objections to the amended petition and that his client has agreed to pay her pro rated fees. Mrs. Pozzi stated that she is good with the amended petition. The Amended Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement, etc. is under submission with the intent to grant. The court noted that the notes for the matter that is on calendar for November 13th need to be cleared. Mrs. Callejo stated that she is not aware of anything else that needs to filed with regard to the petition for probate, that she and the probate examiner do not agree, that she cannot do common ancestry and that she will not be filing anything else on that. Mr. O'Sullivan informed the court that the proposed order does not track the prayer.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Bastain. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-11-2023 Continued To Oct-23-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-31-2023 Continued To Sep-11-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo and Ciaran O'Sullivan appeared by zoom for and with Jonathan Campbell (petitioner) by zoom.
Eddie Broitman appeared by zoom for Janice Pozzi by zoom.

The court asked about the path forward. Mr. O'Sullivan asked the court to set a trial date, stated that they are trying to nail down a deposition date of Mrs. Pozzi and asked for the court's assistance to get a commitment to a deposition date. Mr. O'Sullivan also stated that the issue to be tried is whether the 2018 will is a valid will. Mr. Broitman stated that they are jumping the gun, that the other side hasn't even cleared the notes on their petition, and that procedural issues need to happen first. The court noted that Mrs. Pozzi's petition is okay, that Jonathan's petition is not procedurally ready, and went over the list of items in the probate examiner's notes. Mrs. Callejo stated that they filed amendment number 1 which did not change the petition, that the second amendment changes the date of death, that service of the amendments was done in June, that they filed the proof of service, but it got kicked back and that they will re-file it. Mrs. Callejo also stated that she believes everyone was properly noticed, that she can republish if needed, that she can provide courtesy copies of everything and provide a proposed order. The court informed the parties that it will be issuing a case management order. Mr. Broitman stated that negotiations have been going on up until this morning. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that there is an objection by Mrs. Pozzi regarding the issue of undue influence and so it is at issue. The court stated that it will not set a trial date today. The Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary are continued to September 11, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A case management hearing is also set for that same date. The court noted that informal discovery is available to the parties.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Advanced From Apr-11-23 To Mar-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
Off Calendar Mar-20-2023 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-20-2023 Continued To May-24-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo and Ciaran O'Sullivan appeared by zoom for and with Jonathan Campbell by zoom.
Steven Broitman (former counsel for Rachel Pozzi) appeared by zoom.
Edward Broitman appeared by zoom for and with Rachel Pozzi by zoom.

Mr. Braccini stated that a substitution of attorney was filed last week and asked that his motion to be relieved as counsel be taken off calendar as moot. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that they only heard about substitution last week, that it is time for moving on with this case, and asked the court to set the matter for trial on the issue of the lost will/Probate Code 6124 presumption. The court stated that this doesn't seem like an issue to be tried, and spoke of the circumstances regarding the original will and Mrs. Hinshaw. Mr. Broitman stated that he was hoping to have time to get up to speed and to try to continue with settlement negotiations before going on the trial path. The court stated that both petitions need to be amended, referred to the probate examiner's notes that have been issued on both petitions, stated that they need to get the notes cleared, and noted that the date of death is wrong. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that they have done some written discovery and that they were about depose Mrs. Pozzi. Mrs. Callejo stated that she does not feel her client needs to provide information regarding the relationships/lineage of the distant heirs, and noted that the conservatorship matter is still pending even though the conservatee passed away over two years ago. Mr. Broitman stated that he is not representing Mrs. Pozzi in both matters. The Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (2) are continued to May 24, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court encouraged Mrs. Callejo and Mr. O'Sullivan to reach out to Mr. Broitman.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Continued To Mar-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Continued To Mar-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Steven Braccini, Esq. for and with Janis Rachel Pommon Pozi (Petitioner/Objector), also appearing via Zoom;
Ciaran O'Sullivan, Esq. and Franchesca Callejo, Esq. for and with Jonathan Campbell (Petitioner), also appearing via Zoom;
Caroline Hinshaw, Esq. (Witness).

The Court requested an update. Mr. Braccini stated that this is a will contest, that his firm substituted in, that a subpoena was served on Ms. Hinshaw as the drafting attorney, that they are in the process of written discovery, that his office has filed a motion to be relieved as counsel which is set for April 11th, and that they are requesting a continuance to the end of April. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that they are on the cusp of a settlement and that Mr. Braccini's news of the motion to be relieved as counsel is new and surprising given how long this has gone on and with the second change in counsel. Mr. O'Sullivan requested that the Court advance the motion to be relieved as counsel. The Court stated that it was surprised as well by the motion to be relieved as counsel. Mr. Braccini stated that he is not sure if the motion to be relieved as counsel will be opposed and that there is no objection if the Court wants to advance the motion sua sponte. Ms. Pommon Pozi confirmed for the Court that she will be looking for someone else to represent her in this matter.

At the conclusion of comments, the Court continued both petitions for probate to March 20, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court also advanced the motion to be relieved as counsel from April 11, 2023, to March 20, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: H. Singh. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-31-2022 Continued To Jan-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexandra Banis, via Zoom, for Janis Pozzi, Petitioner; Ciaran O'Sullivan, for and with Jonathan Campbell, Objector; Caroline Hinshaw, Daniel Pommon, Jr., Beneficiary.

The court acknowledges that Ms. Banis has just recently subbed-in for Ms. Pozzi and that it intends to continue the matters set for hearing today to allow her time to work on this matter. Ms. Hinshaw reports of efforts in locating the will and that she doesn't believe that she has any files for Daniel Pommon which is where the will would be located. Counsel and the court discuss the status of discovery in this matter. The court confirms with counsel that both petitions need to be amended to correct the date of death.

After additional discussion, the Status of Mediation and Status of Filing Amended Petition are taken off calendar. Both the Petition for Probate of Will and Petition for Probate of Lost Will are continued to Jan-18-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-06-2022 Continued To Aug-31-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello, via Zoom, for and with Janis Pozzi, Petitioner; Franchesca Callejo and Ciaran O'Sullivan, for and with Jonathan Campbell, Objector.

Mr. O'Sullivan reports that the parties mediated but did not reach a settlement and that the parties are currently working on discovery. The court notes that Ms. Hinshaw did not appear for this matter today and queries counsel regarding the status of the purported lost will. Mr. O'Sullivan does not have a status of the purported lost will and advises that he believes that Ms. Hinshaw has been dealing with some health issues. Mr. O'Sullivan reports that the matter of the lost will should be addressed at an evidentiary hearing so the issue of the lost will may not need to be resolved to move forward. Ms. Robello advises that she believes that the lost will issue should be resolved to avoid any procedural issues and advises that a special administrator should be appointed accordingly. The court offers its position regarding the issue of the lost will. The court confirms with Ms. Robello that the Decedent's date of death is Feb-14-2021 and not Feb-22-2021. The court instructs Ms. Robello to file papers to address that issue. The court points to another issue regarding known heirs. Ms. Robello advises of the filing of a Declaration of Due Diligence Regarding Identification and Notice of Heirs at Law and Ms. Callejo advises that there is also a Declaration Regarding Heirs that has been filed with the court.

After additional discussion, the matters set for hearing on today's calendar are continued to Aug-31-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is advised that trial will not be set at this time but the parties can request a trial date on Aug-31-2022.

Judge: Hon. Lydia Villarreal. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-13-2022 Continued To Jul-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello, via ZOOM, for Rachel Pozzi, via ZOOM, Petitioner; Franchesca Callejo and Ciarian O'Sullivan, via ZOOM, for Jonathan Campbell, via ZOOM, Objector; Caroline Hinshaw, via ZOOM, Interested Party.

Ms. Hinshaw reports that due to health issues, she has been unable to travel to the storage facility to search for the will but that she believes that she'd be able to do so within the next two weeks. Ms. Robello reports that she'd be amenable to participate in pro bono mediation. Ms. Callejo reports that she submitted an amended petition for filing and is pending in the court's e-filing queue. After additional discussion, the Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than Jun-30-2022. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Jul-05-2022. The parties are to return on Jul-06-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. All subjects on today's calendar are continued to the same date. Counsel is to monitor the court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the order re: pro bono mediation.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-02-2022 Continued To Apr-13-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-02-2022 Continued To Apr-13-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello, via ZOOM, for Rachel Pozzi, via ZOOM, Petitioner; Franchesca Callejo and Ciarian O'Sullivan, via ZOOM, for Jonathan Campbell, via ZOOM, Objector; Caroline Hinshaw, via ZOOM, Interested Party.

The court notes that pursuant to the previous calling of this matter, an amended petition has yet to be filed. Ms. Callejo reports of issues determining relationships and that a common ancestor has not been located otherwise, the petition is ready to be filed. Ms. Hinshaw reports that due to health issues she has not been able to search for the will herself but that her business partner has searched via CoroData and they do not have a record of the will but advises that she has yet to search her firm's storage facility but plans on doing so as soon as her health allows. The court points to the issues with the delays in this matter and believed that Ms. Hinshaw would have been more diligent in her efforts to locate the missing will and that the court is considering taking both petitions off calendar. Ms. Robello offers advocacy in support of keeping her petition on calendar and reports that not only is the will missing but the entire file as well. The court and counsel continue to discuss the importance in attempting to locate the missing will. Ms. Hinshaw requests three (3) weeks to search for the will. Ms. Callejo reports that she was hoping that the will had been located prior to filing her amended petition. The court instructs Ms. Hinshaw to advise the parties regarding her efforts to locate the will by close of business on Mar-25-2022. The court advises Ms. Callejo that her amended petition should be filed one week prior to the next calling of this matter.

After additional discussion, the matters on today's calendar are continued to Apr-13-2022 at 9:00 am. A Status of Filing of Amended Petition is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Glisson. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-22-2021 Continued To Mar-02-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello and Jessie Burgueno, via ZOOM, for Rachel Pozzi, via ZOOM, Petitioner; Franchesca Callejo and Ciarian O'Sullivan, via ZOOM, for Jonathan Campbell, Objector.

The court asks Ms. Callejo regarding the status of the amended petition that she mentioned she planned on filing at the previous calling of this matter. Ms. Callejo reports that she plans on filing an amended petition to include heirs within a week or two and that she is seeking litigation counsel. Ms. Callejo also reports waiting for Ms. Hinshaw to confirm the status of the missing will. The court then turns to Ms. Hinshaw for an update regarding the missing will. Ms. Hinshaw reports that the will has not been located and that she plans on searching a storage unit for the will. Mr. Burgueno reports that no evidence has been presented to rebut the presumption under probate code 6124 and requests that the will be admitted, and Ms. Pozzi be appointed Administrator.

After additional discussion, the petitions are continued to Mar-02-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: D. Xiao. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

The court queries counsel regarding whether Ms. Hinshaw has been able to locate the original will. Ms. Callejo reports that the search has been unsuccessful and that an amended petition in response to Examiner's notes is pending and requests a ninety (90) day continuance.
